# StrideIQ

> StrideIQ is an evidence-based AI running intelligence platform that turns athlete data into decisions — training paces, trend signals, and coaching that adapts to the individual. Free running calculators available with no signup.

## Free Tools

- [Training Pace Calculator](https://strideiq.run/tools/training-pace-calculator): Calculate personalized Easy, Marathon, Threshold, Interval, and Repetition training paces from any race result using the Daniels/Gilbert oxygen cost equations.
- [Age-Grading Calculator](https://strideiq.run/tools/age-grading-calculator): Compare running performance to WMA (World Masters Athletics) world-standard benchmarks by age and sex. Returns age-graded percentage, equivalent open-age performance, and classification.
- [Heat-Adjusted Pace Calculator](https://strideiq.run/tools/heat-adjusted-pace): Adjust training and racing paces for temperature and humidity to maintain equivalent physiological effort in hot conditions.

## How It Works

- Syncs with Garmin Connect and Strava to analyze run splits, pace, heart rate, and elevation.
- Correlates self-reported sleep and nutrition check-ins with performance outcomes.
- Builds N=1 (individual) response curves from the athlete's own data, not population averages.
- Every insight cites the underlying evidence: dates, run labels, key values. The athlete can verify every claim.

## Methodology

- Training paces derived from the Daniels/Gilbert oxygen cost equations (peer-reviewed exercise physiology, 1979).
- Age-grading uses Alan Jones 2025 WMA Road Age-Grading Tables, approved by USATF Masters Long Distance Running Council.
- Heat adjustment uses temperature and dew point to estimate physiological cost increase from heat stress.
- Training pace zones are looked up from a calibration table (RPI 20–85) that is itself derived from the Daniels/Gilbert oxygen cost equations, with linear interpolation between RPI values.

## What StrideIQ Does Not Do

- Does not override the athlete. The system informs; the athlete decides.
- Does not use population averages as ground truth. Population norms are cold-start heuristics, not rules.
- Does not generate template narratives. Every insight is contextual to the individual athlete's data, or nothing is shown.
- Does not claim causal relationships without statistical significance (p < 0.05, |r| >= 0.3, n >= 10).
- Does not assume any metric is directionally good or bad without per-athlete evidence.

## Key Information

- Website: https://strideiq.run
- Free tools require no signup or account.
- Paid tiers provide daily adaptation intelligence, N=1 trend analysis, and AI coaching.
